title: BYOP: per-key redirect_uri allowlist + bind client_id to redirect_uri
author: Circuit-Overtime

gist.headline: Safer flights for API keys
gist.blurb: We've upgraded our API key security with a new redirect URI allowlist. Now you can safely bind multiple destinations to a single key without breaking a sweat.
gist.summary: Introduced support for a multiple redirect URI allowlist across the authorization flow, enabling stricter validation and RFC 8252-compliant loopback handling.
gist.impact: Developers can now configure multiple secure redirect URIs per API key through the dashboard, improving security without breaking existing integrations.
gist.visual_concept: API keys, security, redirecting traffic, and allowlists. Visual symbols: keys, signposts/redirect signs, shields/security. Characters: Polly the bee acting as a traffic controller or holding a shiny key with multiple path signs.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny security guard hat, holding a glowing golden API key. She is standing next to a wooden signpost with multiple arrows pointing in different directions, representing redirect URIs. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
